revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Izumo-daijingū]][[ja:出雲大神宮]][[zh:出雲大神宮]] {{Short description|Building in Kameoka, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Izumo Daijingū
出雲大神宮 | image = Izumo-daijingū haiden-2.JPG | caption = Haiden of Izumo Daijingū {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|3|33.86|N|135|34|42.28|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Ōkuninushi; Mihotsu-hime no Mikoto | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = Mubabanchi Chitose Izumo, Chitose-cho, Kameoka-shi, Kyoto-fu 621-0002 | website = {{Official|1=http://www.izumo-d.org}} | architecture_style = | festival = October 21 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Izumo Daijingū'''|出雲大神宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the Chitose neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kameoka, Kyoto|en|Kameoka, Kyoto|qid=Q633996|lt=Kameoka|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|qid=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}}. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the October 21.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Izumo Daijingū are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Ōkuninushi]]'''| 大国主神|}} * {{nihongo|'''Mihotsu-hime no Mikoto'''|三穂津姫尊}}, wife of Ōkuninushi The secondary ''kami'' are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Amatsuhikone]]'''| 天津彦根命|}}, third son of Amaterasu * {{nihongo|'''Takehiratori no Mikoto'''|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|fr}}, wife of Ōkuninushi ==History== The shrine is located at the foot of Mt. Mikage in the eastern part of the Kameoka Basin, which has been worshipped as a {{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|qid=Q1595289|lt=Sacred mountains|1=Sacred mountains}} since ancient times. The origins of Izumo daijingū are unknown. Geographically, the area is in between the two ancient powers of Izumo and Yamato, and the Chitose Kurumazuka Kofun to the southwest of the shrine is the largest keyhole-shape [[Kofun]] in the Tanba region. According to the ''Tanba-koku [[Fudoki]]'', in 709 (during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|qid=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}}), a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the ''kami'' Ōkuninushi was transferred from this shrine to "the land of Kitsuki". For this reason, this shrine is also referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Moto-Izumo'''|元出雲}} ("original Izumo"). The far more famous [[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] was called "Kizuki Shrine" until the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, so until the end of the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the "God of Izumo" referred to Izumo Daijingū. The shrine and its rituals are described in both the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. The shrine is mentioned in the "{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|qid=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}" and ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} In 1292, its Ranking rose to the highest Rank due to success of prayers for rain at this shrine. The "Izumo Shrine" mentioned by [[:en:Yoshida Kenko|Yoshida Kenko]] in the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} ''{{ill|Tsurezuregusa|en|Tsurezuregusa|qid=Q1350535|lt=Tsurezuregusa|1=Tsurezuregusa}}'' (Chapter 236) refers to this shrine. The shrine was extensively reconstructed by {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|qid=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}} in 1345.{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}}, the shrine was rated as a {{nihongo|National shrine, 2nd Rank |国幣中社|[[:en:Kokuhei-chūsha|Kokuhei-chūsha]]}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]].Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The shrine is located a 10-minute walk from either {{ill|Kameoka Station|en|Kameoka Station|qid=Q1043696|lt=Kameoka Station|1=Kameoka Station}} of {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en|Chiyokawa Station|qid=Q1043705|lt=Chiyokawa Station|1=Chiyokawa Station}}. ==Cultural Properties== ===National Important Cultural Properties=== *'''[[Honden]]''', early {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, said to have been renovated by {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|qid=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}} in 1345. It is a ''Sangensha'' style building with a [[Chamaecyparis obtusa|cypress bark]] shingled roof.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/1979|title=出雲大神宮本殿|trans-title=Izumo Daijingu Honden|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} * '''Wooden Statues of male ''kami''''', {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, set of two. These are seated statues carved from {{ill|Torreya nucifera|en|Torreya nucifera|qid=Q1373999|lt=kaya wood|1=Torreya nucifera}}, and date from the 9th or 10th century; one possibly depicts Ōkuninushi, the other is of an unknown ''kami''.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5150|title=木造男神坐像（伝大国主命）|trans-title=Mokuzō danshinzazō (Den ōkuninushimikoto)|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/5151|title=木造男神坐像|trans-title=Mokuzō danshinzazō |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} ==Gallery== Izumo-daijingū mikageyama-3.jpg|Mount Mikage Izumo-daijingū honden-4.jpg|Honden (ICP) File:Izumo-daijingū keidai-3.jpg|Precincts File:Izumo-daijingū haiden-3.JPG|Haiden File:Izumo-daijingū torii-2.JPG|Torii ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}, Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Izumo-daijingū}} *{{official|http://www.izumo-d.org}} {{shinmei shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Izumo-daijingū]] {{Commons category|Izumo-daijingū}} [[ja:出雲大神宮]] [[zh:出雲大神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q10896675}} [[Category:Former Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Independent shrines]] [[Category:Kameoka, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Tanba Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
